claim_bullets.bullet_text: A generator is a function that is defined with function * and uses yield (or yield * ) to generate values. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.245-245)

Using a generator instead of writing an iterator object that has a .next() method allows us to write code that can be much simpler for cases like recursive iterations or state patterns. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.245-245)
And we don't need to worry about wrapping our values in an object with .done and .value properties. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.245-245)

source_record_text: A comprehensive modular guide to JavaScript fundamentals and advanced techniques, spanning 20 chapters.
